design_summary: Small SRAM scratchpad controller with explicit OpenRAM-style memory intent, firmware MMIO register access, and simple BIST status/IRQ collateral. The top module integrates a 64x32 single-port SRAM abstraction and a synthesizable fallback model with identical interface shape for downstream memory-aware synthesis and MBIST generation.

fixed_assumptions: Single synchronous clock domain on clk.
reset_n is a synchronous active-low reset unless later constrained otherwise.
Memory depth is 64 words and memory address width is 6 bits internally, with word-select by MEM_ADDR[5:0].
Firmware-visible MMIO register access is simple and single-cycle from the controller perspective; downstream implementation may pipeline memory accesses as long as register semantics are preserved.
When OpenRAM collateral is available, the controller shall infer or instantiate the memory macro demo_sram_32x64; otherwise it shall use demo_sram_32x64_model with the same external interface.
The top module is the authoritative integration point for register map, memory access, BIST request/status, and interrupt generation.

top_module.name: sram_mbist_demo_controller
top_module.description: Top-level memory scratchpad controller and MBIST demo integration wrapper. Exposes firmware MMIO register control, SRAM read/write access, BIST request/status latching, and IRQ generation while maintaining explicit memory-like structure for downstream tools.
top_module.functionality: Implements a small MMIO register block controlling a 64x32 single-port SRAM scratchpad and a simple BIST request/status interface. Firmware can program memory address, write data, and read data through mapped registers, can request BIST through either the external bist_start input or the register bit in BIST_CONTROL, and can observe or clear BIST status and IRQ state. The design must preserve explicit memory intent through an OpenRAM-compatible SRAM abstraction named demo_sram_32x64 or a fallback synthesizable model named demo_sram_32x64_model.
responsibilities: Decode MMIO-style register accesses from wr_en/wr_addr/wr_data and rd_en/rd_addr.
Expose software-visible control, status, memory address, memory data, memory access control, BIST control, BIST status, IRQ status, and IRQ clear behavior.
Drive and/or monitor the underlying single-port SRAM interface with explicit csb/web/addr/din/dout semantics.
Latch BIST completion and failure indications into status and IRQ registers.
Assert irq only when IRQ_ENABLE is set and a BIST done/fail condition is latched.
Provide deterministic reset behavior for all software-visible registers and outputs.
Preserve a memory-aware hierarchy suitable for OpenRAM-style replacement and MBIST collateral generation.
top_module.reset_behavior: On reset_n deassertion, all control bits are cleared, ready is deasserted until the controller is initialized, BIST done/fail/running state is cleared, IRQ is cleared, MEM_RDATA is cleared to zero, and any latched last_fail_addr is cleared. The reset behavior must not create spurious SRAM writes or spurious BIST start events.
behavior_rules: CONTROL bit 0 enables the controller when set; bit 1 requests a software-visible soft reset of internal state; bit 2 enables IRQ generation from latched BIST status.
STATUS shall reflect ready, bist_done, bist_fail, and busy/running state in a read-only form.
MEM_ADDR holds the 6-bit SRAM address used by subsequent MEM_CONTROL operations.
MEM_WDATA holds the 32-bit write payload for SRAM writes.
MEM_RDATA reflects the last completed SRAM read data and is read-only.
MEM_CONTROL bit 0 triggers a memory write transaction using MEM_ADDR and MEM_WDATA; bit 1 triggers a memory read transaction using MEM_ADDR and captures the returned data into MEM_RDATA.
BIST_CONTROL bit 0 starts BIST through the internal request path; external bist_start shall also request BIST. Bit 1 clears latched BIST result state and IRQ-visible result flags.
BIST_STATUS reports done, fail, running, and last_fail_addr[5:0].
IRQ_STATUS reports latched bist_done and bist_fail indications, and IRQ_CLEAR clears those latched bits when written with one to the corresponding bits.
irq shall assert only when at least one IRQ_STATUS bit is set and the control register IRQ_ENABLE bit is set.
The top module may internally instantiate either demo_sram_32x64 or demo_sram_32x64_model, but the externally visible memory intent must remain explicit and stable.
The controller shall remain synthesizable and avoid vendor-only primitives in the fallback path.

modules.name: demo_sram_32x64
modules.description: OpenRAM-compatible single-port SRAM abstraction used when memory collateral is available. The interface is intentionally macro-like and memory-specific to support downstream replacement, MBIST recognition, and signoff integration.
modules.functionality: Represents a 64-word by 32-bit single-port SRAM macro with active-low chip-select and active-low write-enable. Read data is produced on dout with the expected synchronous macro timing defined by the eventual OpenRAM collateral or compatible memory compiler model.
responsibilities: Provide a stable memory macro abstraction with explicit single-port SRAM semantics.
Support addressable storage depth of 64 and data width of 32.
Allow synthesis and downstream memory replacement flows to recognize a memory-like structure.
Preserve compatibility with the fallback model interface.
modules.reset_behavior: Macro behavioral reset is implementation-defined by the eventual memory collateral; the abstract contract requires that post-reset contents be treated as unknown unless the surrounding controller initializes them.
behavior_rules: csb low enables the port.
web low requests write; web high requests read.
addr selects one of 64 word locations.
din is sampled for writes when csb is active and web indicates write.
dout returns the read data corresponding to the selected address according to the selected implementation timing.
No extra sideband ports are permitted on this abstraction.

modules.name: demo_sram_32x64_model
modules.description: Synthesizable fallback single-port SRAM model matching the OpenRAM-style interface of demo_sram_32x64. Used when macro collateral is unavailable during RTL generation or simulation.
modules.functionality: Implements a synthesizable 64x32 single-port memory with behavior compatible with a typical synchronous SRAM model. It shall infer clean memory structures for synthesis while remaining simple and deterministic for simulation and DFT collateral generation.
responsibilities: Provide a synthesizable fallback memory implementation with the same interface as the macro abstraction.
Ensure the RTL remains portable and free of vendor-specific memory primitives.
Maintain explicit single-port memory intent and 64x32 storage organization.
Support straightforward replacement by an OpenRAM macro during integration.
modules.reset_behavior: The fallback model may clear memory contents or leave them X depending on synthesis/simulation mode, but the surrounding controller must not rely on initialized memory contents unless explicitly written by firmware or BIST.
behavior_rules: Use a single synchronous port with one address, one data input, and one data output.
csb low enables access; web low writes din to the selected address; web high reads the selected address to dout.
Address width is exactly 6 bits and maps to 64 words.
Data width is exactly 32 bits.
The model must be synthesizable and portable.

top_level_connections.description: Top-level clock fans out to the memory abstraction and fallback model; only one implementation is active in the RTL, but both share the same clock contract.
Top active-low reset is consumed by the top controller logic for synchronous reset sequencing.
Firmware write strobe enters the controller register interface.
Firmware write address enters the controller register interface.
Firmware write data enters the controller register interface.
Firmware read strobe enters the controller register interface.
Firmware read address enters the controller register interface.
External BIST start request enters the controller and shall be OR-ed with BIST_CONTROL.START internally.
Top-level register readback data is driven by the controller.
Controller ready indication is driven by the controller.
BIST done indication is driven by the controller status latch.
BIST fail indication is driven by the controller status latch.
Interrupt output is driven by the controller's IRQ generation logic.

inter_module_signals.name: mem_csb
inter_module_signals.description: Active-low memory chip-select generated by the controller for the selected SRAM implementation.

inter_module_signals.name: mem_web
inter_module_signals.description: Active-low memory write-enable generated by the controller for the selected SRAM implementation.

inter_module_signals.name: mem_addr
inter_module_signals.description: Selected SRAM address driven by the controller from MEM_ADDR or BIST sequencing.

inter_module_signals.name: mem_din
inter_module_signals.description: Data written into the selected SRAM location.

inter_module_signals.name: mem_dout
inter_module_signals.description: Read data returned from the active SRAM implementation to the controller.

fields.name: SOFT_RESET
fields.description: Synchronous software reset of controller-visible state.

fields.name: IRQ_ENABLE
fields.description: Enable IRQ generation from latched BIST result status.

fields.name: memory_address
fields.description: Memory address for SRAM access operations.

fields.name: MEM_WRITE
fields.description: Trigger a SRAM write using MEM_ADDR and MEM_WDATA.

fields.name: MEM_READ
fields.description: Trigger a SRAM read using MEM_ADDR and capture data into MEM_RDATA.

fields.name: CLEAR_RESULT
fields.description: Clear latched BIST result and associated IRQ status.

fields.name: clear_bist_done
fields.description: Write one to clear bist_done IRQ status.

fields.name: clear_bist_fail
fields.description: Write one to clear bist_fail IRQ status.
